teamObjective.explanation: I am leading the opening trick, so the goal is to keep control of the trick as cheaply as possible while preserving stronger cards for later.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner has not had any chance to act, so I have no specific signal about partner strength or suit distribution; partner likely holds a roughly random remaining hand.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponents likely hold higher non-trump cards and possibly trumps, since I only have low spades and no trumps, but this is probabilistic because no cards have been played.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Leading the 3 of spades is the lowest-cost lead; it keeps my stronger spades for later tricks and gives partner the best chance to win or cover without committing a high card.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Leads a stronger card unnecessarily; it could win now but wastes a card that might be needed later if opponents pass.
Uses an even stronger card for an opening lead, increasing the chance of winning this trick but reducing my ability to control later tricks.
Wastes my highest spade on a lead when a lower card would achieve the same information-gain and partner-support effect.

risks: An opponent may have a higher non-trump card or any trump and take the trick.
Partner may be unable to cover if the 3 of spades is beaten early.
riskAssessment.mitigation: By leading the lowest card, I preserve the 4, 5, and 6 of spades to compete in later tricks and rely on partner to potentially take this one.
